music.name: SQUISH — Year Anniversary
music.why: Massive Dore Alley Friday headliner — 348 RA interested, Sleep D live set and Two Shell in the same night, 8 hours across The Great Northern
music.summary: SQUISH celebrates its anniversary at The Great Northern on Friday July 24, 8pm–4am, $30–50. One of the most-anticipated SF club nights of the summer, stacking Sleep D (live), Two Shell, PLO Man, SAMO (JP), Club Moniker (llloyd + suade + ryan oh), Just Another Bitch, Manuka Honey, TWINS ONLINE, 1tbsp and Wonja. 21+.

music.name: GRUNT: Dore Saturday
music.why: The Stud's flagship Dore Alley night — leather, sweat, Faited and MS.SMITH; 96 RA interested puts it in the top tier for the weekend
music.summary: GRUNT: Dore Saturday at The Stud on July 25, 9pm–2am, $20. The Stud's leather/queer club night for Up Your Alley weekend, with DJs Faited and MS.SMITH bringing dark electronics and industrial-tinged house to SoMa's most storied queer bar. 21+.

music.name: Up Your Alley / Dore Alley Street Fair & Fetish Festival
music.address: Dore Alley & Folsom St, San Francisco, CA 94103
music.why: SF's grittier, more local leather fair — no ticket, just a suggested donation at the gate
music.summary: The annual Up Your Alley street fair takes over Dore Alley and Folsom Street in SoMa with leather and fetish gear vendors, DJs, gogo dancers, and kink performers. Produced by the nonprofit Folsom Street, it draws roughly 10,000–15,000 attendees as the smaller, more local sibling of September's Folsom Street Fair.

music.name: Mostly Cloudy: Leonce + Syd
music.why: Mostly Cloudy at Monarch stacks an unusually good Saturday bill — Leonce and Syd are exactly the kind of act this room was made for
music.summary: Mostly Cloudy presents Leonce + Syd at Monarch on Saturday July 25, 10pm–3am. Indie dance, queer-forward club sounds on the 6th Street rooftop-adjacent room, with QUEENIE, Casamiento, mymy and Afakasi Papi filling out the bill. 21+.

music.name: ROMP T-Dance: ROMP 69 – The Dirty Discotheque
music.why: Official closing tea dance of Dore Alley weekend, resident DJ spins a seven-hour disco set
music.summary: ROMP T-Dance closes out Dore Alley Weekend with a seven-hour disco-themed set from resident DJ Russ Rich, backed by lasers from RAGERLAZER and visuals from VJ Blanket. The 21+ event enforces a leather/latex/rubber/fetish/glitter dress code and benefits Folsom Street and the Grass Roots Gay Rights Foundation.

music.name: Jody Wisternoff & Nox Vahn (All Night Long)
music.why: Jody Wisternoff and Nox Vahn going b2b all night — two progressive house veterans holding Audio SF for a full run
music.summary: Jody Wisternoff and Nox Vahn play all night long at Audio SF on Friday July 24, 9:30pm–2am. A melodic, progressive house standout with two names who've helped define the form. 21+.

music.name: Soul Clap
music.why: Soul Clap's Dore Alley Saturday at Audio — house with roots in classic soul and disco, always a proper dance experience
music.summary: Soul Clap plays the Dore Alley Saturday slot at Audio SF on July 25, 9:30pm–2am. The Boston duo's blend of classic house, disco and deep funk makes this one of the weekend's more reliable dancefloors. 21+.

music.name: Sam Feldt (Day Party)
music.why: Rare afternoon slot from a festival-circuit headliner — a chance to catch Sam Feldt in a small club instead of a field
music.summary: Dutch dance/pop producer Sam Feldt plays a daytime set at Audio SF on Saturday July 25, 2pm–7pm. Polished melodic house and feel-good electronics, in the intimacy of an 11th Street club. 21+.

music.name: Camp Priceless 2026
music.why: 20th-anniversary underground electronic camping festival deep in the Mendocino redwoods, four days
music.summary: Now in its 20th year, Priceless is a multi-day underground electronic and live music camping festival at Camp Navarro in Mendocino County, July 23–26, spread across Meadow, Lodge and Redwood stages plus a nightly silent disco.

music.name: Death Guild
music.why: World's longest-running weekly goth night, cheap door, 18+, two rooms all night
music.summary: Death Guild, the world's oldest weekly goth/industrial dance night, takes over DNA Lounge on Monday July 20. Six DJs in two rooms — gothic, industrial, synthpop, noise, and ambient — 9:30pm to 2:30am, 18+, $5 before 10:30pm.

music.name: Rhythm in the Sky
music.why: Free rooftop R&B at golden hour — rare weeknight open-air escape downtown
music.summary: DJ Heartbreak's weekly rooftop R&B residency at Timbri Hotel, every Wednesday 5–9pm. Free entry, 21+, golden-hour views over downtown SF.

music.name: Secret Psychedelica: LION HEART with Aktyum
music.why: Public Works' Saturday psychedelic club night during Dore weekend — sliding-scale door makes it the accessible option in an expensive weekend
music.summary: Secret Psychedelica brings LION HEART with Aktyum to Public Works on Saturday July 25, 9pm–2am, $5–25 sliding scale. Techno and deep house across two floors, with MOHARI, Elegie, KAARI, AXL PUERTO and Michael Liu. 21+.

music.name: TUFF: Dore Alley Weekend Kickoff
music.why: The official kickoff to Dore Alley weekend at 1015 — sets the tone for SoMa's leather-and-dance weekend before the street fair
music.summary: TUFF hosts the Dore Alley Weekend Kickoff at 1015 Folsom on Thursday July 23, 9pm–3am, $25–35. The unofficial opener to San Francisco's annual Up Your Alley leather/kink street fair weekend. 21+.

music.name: Deviants Adult Arcade: Up Your Alley Afterparty
music.why: Folsom Street's own multi-room afterparty inside the historic Old Mint building
music.summary: Folsom Street's official Up Your Alley afterparty takes over two floors of the Old Mint with stage takeovers by BOOF and Hot Goth GF, DJs, interactive installations, and live performances. 21+.

music.name: MOVEMENT Ecstatic Dance SF // Medicine Music
music.why: Substance-free sacred-space dance journey with meditation and sound bath, no shoes allowed
music.summary: A substance-free ecstatic dance journey mixed live by DJ Medicine Music at Sacred Heart Church (of 8 Wheels), opening with creative movement and meditation and closing with a sound bath in a shoe-free, sliding-scale-donation space.

music.name: Nightbreed: Horror Rave
music.why: Summerween horror-rave installment with red lighting and horror visuals all night, 18+
music.summary: The second installment of DNA Lounge's Summerween series, Nightbreed: Horror Rave pairs darkwave, industrial, synth and EBM sets from Sage, Melting Girl and Boblivion with red lighting and horror visuals; 18+, 9:30pm–2am.

music.name: No Basic: Octane
music.why: RSVP-only midweek room where each edition is built around one iconic album given a live re-arrangement mid-party; taste-driven, style-forward crowd in a tucked-away Chinatown space
music.summary: No Basic returns to Lion's Den in Chinatown on Wednesday July 22 with resident DJs BAZZ and Jay Neal. Each week the party is guided by an iconic album — this edition gives 'Octane' its moment, a curated set arranged within the night. Doors 10:30pm, 21+, RSVP for the address.

music.name: SF Buddhist Church's 95th Bon Odori Street Dance Festival
music.why: 95th year of one of the oldest, largest Obon dance festivals in the US
music.summary: Community members dance in a circle to taiko drums and folk songs during this traditional Buddhist summer festival, alongside the church's Ginza Bazaar food and craft stalls.

music.name: SOMA Nights: Sidewalk Gallery
music.why: A free SOMA block party turned pop-up outdoor art gallery — a street-level DJ takeover tucked in a hidden Folsom alleyway, no cover.
music.summary: SOMA Nights: Sidewalk Gallery, a free (RSVP) SOMA block party on Thursday July 23, 5–9pm, in a hidden Folsom Street alleyway near 1145 Folsom. Quit Your Day Job brings a pop-up outdoor art gallery and a street-level music takeover; lineup TBA. Part of the SOMA Nights series, RSVP via Partiful.

music.name: Sunset Dance Party
music.why: A free POC/female-led sunset rave on the sand at Ocean Beach — RA-ticketed but no cost, with the exact meetup spot dropped day-of.
music.summary: Sunset Dance Party from No Reason SF, a free sunset rave on the sand at Ocean Beach, Saturday July 25, 3–9pm. Free tickets via Resident Advisor; the precise spot is released day-of. POC- and female-DJ-forward Bay Area crew.

music.name: GENERAIDERZ x NO BIAS: Summer of Wub
music.why: Bass-heavy Dore Alley Friday alternative — GENERAIDERZ and NO BIAS bringing wubs to F8 while the rest of SoMa goes house
music.summary: GENERAIDERZ x NO BIAS: Summer of Wub at F8 on Friday July 24, 10pm–3am, $10–20. Bass music, dubstep and wub-forward club sounds at 1192 Folsom, 21+.

music.name: GROSSOMODDO: Mediterranean Afro House
music.why: Mediterranean and African rhythms in the Madarae basement — the sleeper pick for the weekend if big rooms feel too crowded
music.summary: GROSSOMODDO brings Mediterranean Afro House to Madarae SF (46 Minna St) on Saturday July 25, 9pm–3am. Percussive, sun-drenched African and Mediterranean house sounds in an intimate basement setting, 21+.

music.name: Free Reggae in the Park: Crucial Sundays
music.venue: Spreckels Temple of Music (Golden Gate Park Bandshell)
music.address: Music Concourse Dr, San Francisco, CA
music.why: Free all-ages reggae showcase returns weekly to the historic Golden Gate Park bandshell stage
music.summary: Part of the Illuminate LIVE free concert series, this Crucial Sundays reggae session brings DJ Crossfire, DJ Guid8nce, DJ Sep, Irie Dole and King Tappa to the Golden Gate Park Bandshell, 4:30–7:30pm, free and all ages.

music.name: Funk Stop Day Party
music.why: A free RSVP East Bay day party on Telegraph Ave — house, techno, breaks and bass all afternoon with a live DJ contest, dancers and fire performers, no cover.
music.summary: Funk Stop Day Party, a free (RSVP) East Bay day party at Club Cali in Berkeley, Saturday July 25, 2–9pm. House, techno, breaks and bass across the afternoon with a live DJ contest, dancers, fire performers and local vendors; lineup TBA. RSVP via Posh.

music.name: Interzone Darkwave Tuesdays & Las Hechiceras
music.why: Recurring midweek darkwave refuge in SoMa — EBM and goth for $5 on a Tuesday, now with Las Hechiceras as guest
music.summary: Interzone Darkwave Tuesdays at F8 on Tuesday July 21, 9pm–2am, $5 — the weekly EBM/darkwave/goth club night on Folsom, this week featuring Las Hechiceras as guest DJs alongside the Hex Embrace residents. 21+.

music.name: Strut SF presents Bizen Lopez
music.why: Strut SF's midweek queer house night with a guest import from beyond the Bay — intimate and community-rooted
music.summary: Strut SF and F8 present Bizen Lopez on Wednesday July 22 at 1192 Folsom, 9pm–2am, $10. A queer-focused house night at one of SoMa's cornerstone spaces. 21+.

music.name: Golden Gate Park Band: Symphony in the Park — A Bonnie Scottish Celebration
music.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118
music.why: 144-year-old community band pairs live Highland dance with Scottish marches and airs
music.summary: The Golden Gate Park Band celebrates the spirit and pageantry of Scotland with marches, airs, and Highland-inspired rhythms, joined onstage by the Dunsmuir Scottish Dancers.

music.name: Lindy in the Park Swing Dance Party
music.venue: JFK Drive Lindy Hop Spot, Golden Gate Park
music.address: JFK Dr between 8th Ave & 10th Ave (behind the de Young Museum), San Francisco, CA 94118
music.why: Longest-running free outdoor swing dance party in the Bay Area, running since 1996
music.summary: Free social swing dancing on car-free JFK Drive, opening with a beginner lesson from Hep Jen followed by open dancing to jazz, blues and swing until 2pm, weather permitting.

music.name: Brown Suga: A Neo Soul & R&B Experience
music.why: A waterfront Neo Soul & R&B day party with a curated shades-of-brown dress code and a signature cocktail menu — a dressier, sit-down alternative to the usual club day party.
music.summary: DJ SM and Heartbroken Entertainment Group present Brown Suga, a Neo Soul & R&B waterfront day party at Eria Marina, Sunday July 26, 3-8pm, shades-of-brown dress code.

music.name: Broken Promises: Walnut Creek Takeover
music.address: 1515 N California Blvd, Walnut Creek, CA 94596
music.why: Full two-floor venue takeover from DJ Heartbreak's Broken Promises brand returning to the city where it started, dress code strictly enforced
music.summary: Broken Promises returns to 1515 Bar & Restaurant in Walnut Creek for a full venue takeover with DJ Heartbreak & Friends, Saturday July 25, 9pm–1am. Two floors of all-R&B. 21+, dress code strictly enforced. Early ticket purchase recommended.

music.name: PHREAK-OUT
music.address: Middle Harbor Shoreline Park, Oakland, CA
music.why: Cyberpunk-themed outdoor jungle/DnB rave with open decks at noon and a 7-hour, 7-act lineup including UK import Omnirhythm
music.summary: PHREAK-OUT is an outdoor jungle/DnB/neurofunk/dubstep/UKG/house rave at Middle Harbor Shoreline Park in Oakland, Saturday July 25, 12pm–7pm. Open decks at noon, live aerial and live art, BBQ by Chef Matt Ettl. Lineup: Ada Lovebass, Bloodshed, Destrukshin, MC Enhale, Omnirhythm (UK), Shay-D-Beats, 4 Dollar Dolphins. Presented by The Amalgam Collective, Calling All Ravers & Deep Legends.

sports.name: Marin Roller Disco Daze
sports.address: 10 Avenue of the Flags, San Rafael, CA 94903
sports.why: Free outdoor neon roller rink from SF's Church of 8 Wheels, runs Fri–Sun
sports.summary: Marin Center and Church of 8 Wheels turn Lagoon Island at the Marin County Fairgrounds into a free outdoor roller disco, open through mid-August; this Friday runs noon–10pm with $5 skate rentals, lawn games, and a giant inflatable skate.

sports.name: Salsa Night with Live Band: Pepe Y Su Orquesta
sports.why: Live salsa band Pepe Y Su Orquesta plus lesson, weekly North Beach staple
sports.summary: Cigar Bar & Grill's weekly Friday salsa night opens with a 7pm lesson from Ava & Rodolfo, then live band Pepe Y Su Orquesta takes over from 9:30pm; $20 cover after 9pm, 21+, dress code enforced.

sports.name: Fog City Run (Wednesday 5K)
sports.why: A $5 timed 5K through the hilly Marina every Wednesday — the cheapest racing buzz in the city
sports.summary: Fog City Run hosts a weekly hilly 5K race starting from The Blue Light bar at 1979 Union St in SF's Marina district on Wednesday July 22 at 6:30pm, rain or shine; arrive by 6:15pm, $5.

sports.name: Sunset Dunes parkrun 5K
sports.address: 1349 Upper Great Hwy, San Francisco, CA 94122
sports.why: Free timed 5K on the coastal dunes with ocean views — Saturday's best zero-cost athletic start
sports.summary: Sunset Dunes parkrun hosts a free weekly timed 5K at Sunset Dunes Park on Upper Great Hwy in SF on Saturday July 26 at 8am; register free at parkrun.us, all paces welcome, all ages.

sports.name: Women & Non-Binary Bike SF: Friday Morning Ride
sports.why: Social-pace ride for women & non-binary riders, slow enough to talk
sports.summary: SF Bicycle Coalition's Women & Non-Binary Friday Morning Ride meets at McLaren Lodge in Golden Gate Park, departing around 7:30am and returning by 8:45am at an easy conversational pace.

sports.name: 510 Run to Beers
sports.why: 2.6-mile Oakland loop that ends with craft beer on Temescal's outdoor patio — untimed, no pressure
sports.summary: 510 Run to Beers meets at Temescal Brewing Co. at 4115 Telegraph Ave in Oakland on Thursday July 24 at 6:30pm for a free ~2.6-mile group run to Piedmont Ave and back; all paces welcome, beers after.

sports.name: Berkeley Running Club: Tilden Park Trail Run
sports.why: Hilly 5–10 mile Tilden trails with a flexible group — the real runner's pick if flat roads bore you
sports.summary: Berkeley Running Club meets at Tilden Park (Lone Oak) on Tuesday July 21 at 6:30pm for a free hilly trail run of 5–10 miles depending on pace group; all are welcome, self-sufficient runners preferred.

sports.name: Marin Running Club: Thursday Trail Run
sports.address: 703 Tennessee Valley Rd, Mill Valley, CA 94941
sports.why: Mid-week trail escape from SF — Tennessee Valley to the ocean and back, free and untimed
sports.summary: Marin Running Club meets at Tennessee Valley Trailhead in Mill Valley on Thursday July 24 at 6pm for a free group trail run with distances and pace groups to suit all levels; self-sufficient runners.

sports.name: Marin Running Club: Tuesday Bunny Run
sports.address: 703 Tennessee Valley Rd, Mill Valley, CA 94941
sports.why: Beginner-paced 30-minute trail intro at a gorgeous Marin trailhead — slowest and most welcoming run of the week
sports.summary: Marin Running Club's Tuesday Bunny Run meets at Tennessee Valley Trailhead in Mill Valley on Tuesday July 21 at 6pm, a free beginner-friendly 30-minute trail run at a leisurely pace; all welcome.

sports.name: Mission 5K Fun Run
sports.why: Casual Monday neighborhood run that ends at a bar — social first, athletic second
sports.summary: The Mission Fun Runs group meets at Teeth Bar in the Mission on Monday July 20 at 6:30pm for a free social 5K run through the neighborhood; all paces welcome, drinks after.

sports.name: Mission Fun Runs: Friday Morning Pastry Run @ Bageletto
sports.why: Post-run bagels replace the usual bar stop, easygoing morning pace
sports.summary: Mission Fun Runs hosts a free Friday morning pastry run from Bageletto, following a pre-set route before returning for bagels; all paces welcome.

sports.name: Oakland Beer Runners: Lake Merritt Run
sports.why: Free Tuesday jog around Lake Merritt followed by craft beer — running optional, community the point
sports.summary: Oakland Beer Runners meets at Original Pattern Brewing Co. on Tuesday July 21 at 6:30pm for a free group run around Lake Merritt (approx 3 miles); all paces welcome, drinks at the brewery after.

sports.name: Polo Field 5K (DSE Runners)
sports.address: Middle Dr W & Metson Rd, San Francisco, CA
sports.why: Free Sunday 5K inside Golden Gate Park from a club that's been running the same loop for decades
sports.summary: DSE Runners hosts a free weekly Polo Field 5K in Golden Gate Park on Sunday July 26 at 9am, starting at the polo fields; open community run, all paces welcome.

sports.name: SFRRC Wednesday Fun Run in the Presidio
sports.address: 610 Old Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94129
sports.why: Scenic Presidio loop with an organized club — best midweek views in SF for zero cost
sports.summary: SF Road Runners Club hosts a free weekly Wednesday fun run from Sports Basement Presidio on July 22 at 6pm, with routes through the Presidio trails and park roads; all paces welcome.

sports.name: Sunday Funday Ride — Livermore to San Jose
sports.why: A full-day backroads loop out past the Altamont and Central Valley edge — a proper ride, not a coffee-and-back meetup
sports.summary: Sunday group motorcycle ride from Livermore out to Patterson and back into San Jose, roughly a 6-hour loop, on Sunday July 26. Free to join.
